response.justification: The role demands 8 years of C++ programming plus large-scale cloud-compute-platform leadership, and the candidate meets almost none of the hard requirements. C++ exposure is limited to roughly six months at National Instruments (2014–2015) and a bachelor thesis — far short of 8 years. The candidate's depth is in JavaScript/TypeScript and DLang full-stack work, with some relevant distributed/low-latency experience (Dunnhumby) and strong teaching/mentoring history, but no production cloud architecture ownership or formal technical-leadership role over cloud/DevOps engineers. The profile is a capable generalist craftsman, not a match for this C++-centric platform lead.
strengths: Distributed, high-throughput systems experience (millions of requests/sec at Dunnhumby)
Strong mentoring, teaching, and engineering-practice background (TDD, CI/CD, workshops)
Broad, long full-stack engineering tenure across many domains
concerns: Far below the 8-year C++ requirement (only ~6 months of C/C++ work)
No production cloud-architecture or cloud-compute-platform leadership experience
No formal technical-leadership role mentoring cloud/DevOps engineers
